steps.title: Identify what causes barter's inefficiency according to Champ and Freeman
steps.content: The key signal is their explanation of barter's core problem: both parties must want what the other has (“the person with whom you wish to trade must not only have what you want but also want what you have”). This mutual requirement is what Champ and Freeman pinpoint as the source of inefficiency.

steps.title: Find the paraphrase that captures this explanation
steps.content: Choice B paraphrases Champ and Freeman's explanation by framing this mutual requirement as “the precise alignment of desires.” The other choices either just restate the definition of barter (A and C) or shift to how barter becomes monetary (D), rather than explaining what makes barter inefficient. That matches choice B — B is correct.
